compiled_state.title: [Worktree Isolation]: Agent dispatched with isolation='worktree' -> writes files to session branch instead of isolated worktree
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
Agent dispatched with isolation='worktree' (Agent tool parameter explicitly set) created files in the orchestrator's session branch working directory instead of its isolated worktree directory.

Observed: Agent a5a2e497 was dispatched for story 6121-46dc with isolation='worktree'. Its assigned worktree was /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-a5a2e497. However, the agent wrote all files to the session branch directory /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra-worktrees/worktree-20260416-132412/ — the orchestrator's CWD — instead.

Files written to session branch (should have gone to isolated worktree):
- plugins/dso/agents/cross-epic-interaction-classifier.md (NEW)
- plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/prompts/cross-epic-scan.md (NEW)
- tests/skills/test-brainstorm-cross-epic-scan.sh (NEW)
- plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md (MODIFIED)
- .test-index (MODIFIED)
- plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (MODIFIED)

Impact: Blocked harvest of other worktrees (e4c5-3aad) because session branch had unstaged changes to files the merge needed to update (.test-index). Required committing session branch changes directly instead of via harvest-worktree.sh protocol.

Expected: With isolation='worktree', the agent should operate entirely within its assigned worktree path and never touch the orchestrator's working directory.

comments.body:
Classification: behavioral (platform isolation behavior). Score: severity=1, complexity=2, environment=0, flaky=1 (suspected non-determinism — evidence shows isolation sometimes works, sometimes not) = 4 → INTERMEDIATE tier.

Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed.

Investigation scope: (a) empirically reproduce isolation:worktree behavior in this session, (b) determine whether the platform enforces isolation consistently, (c) identify what conditions cause agents to write to orchestrator CWD instead of the isolated worktree. Investigation feeds epic c737-977d approach selection.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
INTERMEDIATE investigation complete (confidence: medium→revised after empirical testing).

EMPIRICAL TEST RESULTS (2026-04-16, parallel dispatch, same session):

Control (isolation omitted):
- sub-agent PWD: orchestrator root (same as caller)
- sub-agent git rev-parse: orchestrator root
- canary-NOISO.txt created: landed in orchestrator session tree

Isolated (isolation='worktree'):
- sub-agent PWD: /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.claude/worktrees/agent-ad25e139
- sub-agent git rev-parse: same isolated path
- canary-ISO.txt created: landed in isolated worktree, NOT in orchestrator session
- Agent result returned worktreePath + worktreeBranch metadata

VERDICT: Platform isolation WORKS RELIABLY in this environment. be6c-ed89's observed file leak cannot be reproduced in controlled conditions today. Hypothesis that 'isolation is inconsistently enforced at the platform layer' is DISPROVED for the current build.

Residual root-cause candidates for the original observation on story 6121-46dc:
(1) The dispatched sub-agent's prompt embedded a cd into the orchestrator root, breaking CWD-based isolation.
(2) The prompt contained absolute paths (e.g., to /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra-worktrees/worktree-20260416-132412/...) that bypassed CWD — writes followed the path, not the CWD.
(3) User misattribution — files may have been in the isolated worktree but appeared in the session after a merge/harvest.
(4) A transient platform regression that has since been fixed.

Investigation sub-agent's other confirmed finding: commit 65ffaa44 removed hook_worktree_isolation_guard (the only pre-dispatch enforcement layer). No current hook validates isolation mode. ORCHESTRATOR_ROOT is NOT auto-injected by the platform — it must be added to each dispatch prompt manually. Sprint's task-execution.md has no {orchestrator_root} placeholder.

Recommendation: bug behavior cannot be reproduced; investigation outputs should feed epic c737-977d approach decision rather than drive a standalone fix. Options for be6c-ed89 lifecycle:
(a) Close as unreproducible with this investigation comment as evidence.
(b) Leave open pending defensive fix in epic c737-977d (template enforcement + post-dispatch verification).
(c) Close after a targeted defensive change lands (Fix 1 from investigation: add {orchestrator_root} placeholder to dispatch templates and fail-closed when absent).
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Disposition: folded into epic c737-977d (defense-in-depth scope). Investigation confirmed platform isolation works reliably today; the observed file leak cannot be reproduced. The epic will land template enforcement + post-dispatch verification + working-tree snapshot-diff, which also addresses this ticket. Ticket stays open until that epic closes; no standalone code change planned.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Triaged (debug-everything): orchestration/worktree bug. Fix spans multiple scripts; autonomous scope too broad. Deferred to end-of-session user review.
comments.author: Test
